records.ety: [Fr. Gr. <?/ removing all sorrow; hence, an epithet of an Egyptian drug which lulled sorrow for the day; <?/ not + <?/ sorrow, grief.]

defs: A drug used by the ancients to give relief from pain and sorrow; -- by some supposed to have been opium or hasheesh. Hence, anything soothing and comforting.
quotes: Lulled with the sweet nepenthe of a court.
Quaff, O quaff this kind nepenthe.
